children.heading: Agent
children.label: 1 Agent
children.text: The term “agent” means a nuclear, biological, chemical, or radiological substance that causes agricultural disease or the adulteration of products regulated by the Secretary of Agriculture under any provision of law.

children.label: A
children.text: plant or animal health;

children.label: B
children.text: public health as it relates to the adulteration of products regulated by the Secretary of Agriculture under any provision of law that is caused by exposure to an agent; or

children.label: C
children.text: the environment as it relates to agriculture facilities, farmland, and air and water within the immediate vicinity of an area associated with an agricultural disease or outbreak.

children.label: A
children.text: means a product, practice, or technology that is intended to enhance or maintain the agricultural biosecurity of the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: does not include a product, practice, or technology used solely in response to a human medical incident or public health emergency not related to agriculture.

children.heading: Agricultural disease
children.label: 4 Agricultural disease
children.text: The term “agricultural disease” has the meaning given the term by the Secretary.

children.heading: Agricultural disease emergency
children.label: 5 Agricultural disease emergency
children.text: The term “agricultural disease emergency” means an incident of agricultural disease that requires prompt action to prevent significant damage to people, plants, or animals.

children.label: i
children.text: damage to agriculture; or

children.label: ii
children.text: injury to a person associated with agriculture; and

children.label: i
children.text: intimidate or coerce a civilian population; or

children.label: ii
children.text: disrupt the agricultural industry in order to influence the policy of a government by intimidation or coercion.

children.heading: Animal
children.label: 7 Animal
children.text: The term “animal” has the meaning given the term in .section 8302 of this title

children.heading: Department
children.label: 8 Department
children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Agriculture.

children.label: A
children.text: research leading to the identification of products or technologies intended for use as agricultural countermeasures to protect animal health;

children.label: B
children.text: the formulation, production, and subsequent modification of those products or technologies;

children.label: C
children.text: the conduct of in vitro and in vivo studies;

children.label: D
children.text: the conduct of field, efficacy, and safety studies;

children.label: E
children.text: the preparation of an application for marketing approval for submission to an applicable agency; or

children.label: F
children.text: other actions taken by an applicable agency in a case in which an agricultural countermeasure is procured or used prior to issuance of a license or other form of Federal Government approval.

children.heading: Plant
children.label: 10 Plant
children.text: The term “plant” has the meaning given the term in .section 7702 of this title

children.heading: Qualified agricultural countermeasure
children.label: 11 Qualified agricultural countermeasure
children.text: The term “qualified agricultural countermeasure” means an agricultural countermeasure that the Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Homeland Security, determines to be a priority in order to address an agricultural biosecurity threat.
